Behind the rising prices of phones isn't inflation—it's the AI's memory appetite. In short: the RAM paradox.

The mechanism is simple but ruthless:

Manufacturers produce either HBM for AI or standard memory for phones from the same wafer; the two can't happen simultaneously.

Everyone’s calling an AI bubble. Meanwhile China’s AI server market is going ~80% domestic in 2026, with Nvidia + AMD dropping from 34% to 21% (SCMP/TrendForce).

The demand is real. It’s just splitting into two ecosystems. Most valuations are still priced for one.

“Delaying will bring prices down”. 

Wrong:

Micron 5-year contracts: Micron locked major customers into take-or-pay memory supply deals running through 2030, signaling buyers expect shortages to last.

$100B minimum revenue: Micron’s long-term agreements represent about $100B in
